description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Add a Daytona-backed Den Docker dev flow, including the new den-v2 service set, worker proxy, DB package, and provisioning/e2e scripts.
- Improve Den org and environment handling so local/dev setups sync more reliably and generate unique org slugs.
Fixes & polish
- Fix webdev:local so the local web script starts reliably from the helper script.
- Remove stale sidebar screenshot artifacts from the repo and carry the 0.11.166 version bump across app, desktop, orchestrator, server, and router packages.
Packaging / ops
- Add a macOS arm64 alpha artifact workflow.
- Update the AUR packaging metadata for the prior release line before shipping 0.11.166.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.165...v0.11.166
